export default function MDXContent({

  return <MDXLayout {...layoutProps} {...props} components={components} mdxType="MDXLayout">


    <h1 {...{
      "id": "add-lifecycle-hooks"
    }}>{`Add Lifecycle Hooks`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn how to respond to plugin and panel events`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`As we have seen in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../concepts/entrypoints/index.md#plugin-lifecycle-hooks"
      }}>{`Entrypoints concept guide`}</a>{`, lifecycle hooks are callback functions that are automatically invoked when specific events occur during the lifecycle of a Plugin or a Panel. They are optional and can be used to perform, for example, any setup or teardown logic.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`This guide is only `}<strong parentName="p">{`applicable to Plugins with at least one Panel`}</strong>{`; there are no hooks related to Commands. Read `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../concepts/panels-and-commands/index.md#key-differences"
      }}>{`here`}</a>{` if you need a refresher on the difference between a Command and a Panel.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "hook-types"
    }}>{`Hook Types`}</h2>
    <p>{`UXP provides two kinds of lifecycle hooks:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`At a `}<strong parentName="li">{`Plugin level`}</strong>{`, respectively invoked when the plugin container itself is created and destroyed: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`At a `}<strong parentName="li">{`Panel level`}</strong>{`, respectively invoked when the panel is created, shown, hidden, and destroyed: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`More details on the types of hooks can be found in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../concepts/entrypoints/index.md#plugin-lifecycle-hooks"
      }}>{`Entrypoints concept guide`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="error" slots="header, text, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Current limitations`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{` hooks are `}<strong parentName="p">{`not working as expected yet`}</strong>{` in Premiere; this will be fixed in a future update.`}</p>
    <p>{`For plugins with multiple panels, `}<strong parentName="p">{`lifecycle hooks currently fire for all panels`}</strong>{` without distinguishing between them`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "implementation"
    }}>{`Implementation`}</h2>
    <p>{`Hooks are implemented in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{` method, which accepts an object with the following properties:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin`}</inlineCode>{`: An object with the plugin lifecycle hooks.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`panels`}</inlineCode>{`: An object with one property for each `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"panel"`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoint declared in the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`—using the panel IDs as property names, and objects with the panel lifecycle hooks as values.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Promises are allowed for most callbacks, as demonstrated in the following example. Panel hooks receive a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`rootNode`}</inlineCode>{` parameter; it's the HTML document root node, which can be used to programmatically append or remove an appropriate container element in a multiple panels scenario.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example"
    }}>{`Example`}</h3>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");
entrypoints.setup({
  plugin: {
    create() {
      console.log("Plugin create hook");
    },
    destroy() {
      return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
        console.log("Plugin destroy hook");
        resolve();
      });
    },
  },
  panels: {
    firstPanel: { // 👈 matches the panel ID from manifest.json
      create(rootNode) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel create hook", rootNode);
          resolve();
        });
      },
      show(rootNode, data) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel show hook", data);
          resolve();
        });
      },
      hide(rootNode, data) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel hide hook", data);
          resolve();
        });
      },
      destroy(rootNode) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel destroy hook", rootNode);
          resolve();
        });
      },
    },
  },
});

`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "entrypoints": [
    {
      "type": "panel",
      "id": "firstPanel",
      "label": "My plugin",
      "minimumSize": { "width": 400, "height": 400 },
      "maximumSize": { "width": 800, "height": 800 },
      "preferredDockedSize": { "width": 400, "height": 400 },
      "preferredFloatingSize": { "width": 600, "height": 600 }
    }
  ],
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <p>{`Lifecycle hooks are callback functions that automatically execute during specific plugin and panel events, enabling setup and teardown logic for UXP plugins in Adobe Premiere.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Key Concepts:`}</strong></p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Plugin-level hooks`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{` are triggered when the plugin container is created/destroyed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Panel-level hooks`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{` are triggered during panel lifecycle events.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Implementation`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`plugin`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`panels`}</inlineCode>{` objects to define hooks. Promises are allowed for most callbacks.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Known limitations`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{` hooks are not working as expected in Premiere (will be fixed in future updates). For multi-panel plugins, lifecycle hooks currently fire for all panels without distinction.`}</li>
    </ol>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
MDXContent.isMDXComponent = true;
